title: Durable Due Scheduling, Clock Injection, Timers, Backoff, and Pause · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for durable due scheduling, clock injection, timers, backoff, and pause and accepts only runtime and compiler evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, Node, network, XML, PostgreSQL, handle, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for durable handle-clean adaptive scheduler. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Persist next attempt, last success, failure streak, publisher guidance, pause cause, and schedule reason outside process timers.
Inject clock and sleep ports, distinguish elapsed from civil time, define boundary equality, and test skew and jumps.
Schedule the next bounded timer only after ownership and settlement, unref deliberately, clear on close, and expose handle evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
